steps.title: Determine the multiplication factor when w increases by 1
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, the exponent $3w$ increases by 3. So:

$$z(w+1) = (0.835)^{3(w+1)} = (0.835)^{3w+3} = (0.835)^{3w} \cdot (0.835)^3 = z(w) \cdot (0.835)^3$$

The function value is multiplied by $(0.835)^3$.

steps.title: Convert the multiplication factor to a percentage decrease
steps.content:
A percentage decrease of $p$% means the new value equals the old value times $(1 - \frac{p}{100})$. Since the value is multiplied by $(0.835)^3$:

$$(0.835)^3 = 1 - \frac{p}{100}$$

Computing $(0.835)^3$: We have $(0.835)^2 = 0.697225$, so $(0.835)^3 = 0.697225 \times 0.835 \approx 0.5822$.

Solving for $p$:

$$p = 100(1 - 0.5822) = 100 \times 0.4178 = 41.78 \approx 41.8$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
